content.claim: Poland delayed tracking from age 15 to 16 in 1999 reform and subsequently showed improved PISA outcomes and 3% employment and 4% earnings gains, but most changes were reversed after 2016
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Reform was multidimensional (not tracking age alone); other simultaneous changes confound attribution
Reversal occurred for political, not evidential, reasons; does not resolve whether delayed tracking itself was causal
Time lag between reform and measurement (20 years) introduces numerous co-occurring macroeconomic factors
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
